title: RFC: Naming refactor and tests
body:
This is motivated by the outstanding comments in #1085, but I’m really only starting to build the confidence I need to touch that code. Still, it is independent enough to perhaps be merged, and at least to gather comments about this work.

So, for now there should be exactly one functional change: supporting all kinds of digests instead of only `@sha256:` in `imageParts`. The rest is:

- renames for clarity and consistency
- splitting functions into stateless and easily-testable and the storage-dependent environment handling
- hopefully comprehensive unit tests for `imageParts`, `TagImage` input normalization, and interpreting non-_transport_`:` inputs for `pullImage`.

Please see individual commit messages for more details.

**RFC**:
- Is this wanted at all? Or should I just leave working-enough code alone, and not slow everyone down with merge conflicts? (FWIW at least some of the `FIXME` comments are real bugs.)
- Is structuring the PR as a series of small commits acceptable?
- AFAICT the outcome of #1085, apart from pulling the right image, which is clearly correct, is that pulled _name_`@sha256:`_digest_ images are named in the storage as _name_`:none` (where `none` is an actual tag string, not “missing”, and the digest not recorded). (I can now confidently refactor the code to do this inside the `imageParts` abstraction without breaking anything, but that exposes how irregular the code doing this is.) 
  - Is using the explicit `none` tag  Is that intentional? If so, why is it necessary? 
  - Is dropping the digest and not recording it in the storage intentional? If so, why is it necessary? (Looking at `Image.RepoDigests`, it forms a series of _name_`@`_digest_ values, all with the same `Image.image.Digest` value — but a single image (single image ID == config digest) can exist with multiple different manifest digests. Shouldn’t the original values be recorded when pulling, and reproduced in `RepoDigests` in `podman inspect`?)

comments.body:
I personally have no issue with clarity refactors (actually planning a good number myself once things settle down code-wise), so I have no issue moving forward with this.

As to the rest of your questions, I'll have to defer to @baude who wrote the relevant pieces of code.

Proper review coming tomorrow, hopefully.
Changes look fine, though I'd like to get the `:none` situation sorted with @baude before we consider a merge
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1103) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

> Changes look fine, though I'd like to get the :none situation sorted with @baude before we consider a merge

Note that this PR does not change the `none` situation at all (it only adds tests which reflect the current behavior). The complexity of the `none` semantics is exactly why this PR stops just before that.

> Is this wanted at all? Or should I just leave working-enough code alone, and not slow everyone down with merge conflicts? (FWIW at least some of the FIXME comments are real bugs.)

ACK from my side. Notice that I don't have a merge conflict with the proposed changes, but they improve the quality and maintainability of the code. The `FIXME`s also add quite some working items. Not sure, if those should eventually be turned into github issues.

> Is structuring the PR as a series of small commits acceptable?

Big ACK from my side. That's how an ideal PR looks like (in my opinion). Github somehow discourages doing that but reviewing your PR was extremely comfortable.

LGTM on the code.

I cannot answer the remaining questions. @baude should have answers for those.

@mtrmac If the open questions are not directly related to this PR, I'm fine merging if you take off the RFC label (Homu refuses to merge WIP/RFC PRs). Could you open an issue about it to make sure the conversation isn't lost, though?

LGTM
The multiple Patches is fine with me.  It is easier to review that way.
I will let @baude answer the specific quesitons.  But I wil lmerge now.

@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit 96486f8 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 96486f81ea99261d321958261a9885ef9531e299 -->
Oops I missed the RFC on the github,  We'll see if Homu refuses.

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/8ff0eb53cb7bc1e38ce784ce71e44d03e0210af3.1532351976538590720/index.html)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing 8ff0eb53cb7bc1e38ce784ce71e44d03e0210af3 to master...

> Could you open an issue about it to make sure the conversation isn't lost, though?

Thanks, filed #1139; let’s discuss there.
